what_it_does: Designs render rules and a live interactive cognition view for the concurrent-cognition layer, mapping registry-defined roles, chains, per-turn runs, injections, configs, outputs, and destinations to visual primitives (nodes, edges, turn frames, status tokens) and specifying the backend emit contracts and frontend reuse/net-new components needed to surface it.
observations: It is the rendering chapter (06) of the concurrent-cognition work, marked open/provisional/design surface.
It binds render rules to the data model in the vault spine and the live ROLE_REGISTRY / context_variables.REGISTRY rather than inventing a new model.
It treats the canvas as a generic reflects-never-owns renderer and positions the cognition view as a sibling projection, not new machinery.
It includes a mapping table from cognition data (role, chain, turn, injection, config, output, destination) to visual primitives and render rules.
It proposes a registry-defined cognition node-state vocabulary: latent, firing, ran, injected, failed, abstained.
It lists net-new backend work first (cognition registry, per-turn emissions, node states, ui:// rows) and calls the event emit contract the view's hard dependency.
It lists net-new frontend work (CognitionView region, RoleShape, cognition Edges, SSE dispatch branch, registry-store fields).
It ends with open questions explicitly deferred to Tim, including own-surface vs overlay, persistence, address scheme, and aesthetic tokens.
It notes the directory only contained this 06 task; 01-05 are conceptual in the vault.
standouts: The view is driven entirely by addressed SSE cognition.* events; the file states the design floats until the backend emits them.
It proposes reusing NodeShape/Edges/registryStore patterns so roles, chains, and injections render generically from registry data.
It makes the brain node the target of injection edges and treats the brain-to-chat hand-off as a separate terminal cue, not a shape-to-shape edge.
conventions: Uses epistemic tags: Observed, Designed, Open.
Cites file:line locations and vault spine references for grounding.
Follows reflects-never-owns, registry-driven UI, one-source, fail-loud, and navigable-visual-surface principles.
Structures the doc as orientation, substrate, data model, design, reuse, net-new, reuse spine, open questions, and sources.
Acknowledges AGENTS.md rules 3, 4, 8, and 9 as governing.
Notes the directory numbering gap honestly instead of pretending 01-05 files exist.
concerns: The design depends on several unbuilt backend pieces (cognition registry, per-turn event emissions, node-state registration) so it cannot be implemented from this file alone.
It floats a possible new cog:// address scheme while flagging it as a CONFIRM-level contract decision, leaving a design tension unresolved.
Many key UX decisions (surface placement, persistence, tokens) are deferred to Tim, making the file highly provisional.
The mismatch between the 06 filename and the absence of 01-05 files in the repo could confuse future readers.
notes: A provisional render-design chapter that maps the concurrent-cognition data model to a live node/edge canvas and explicitly lists the unbuilt backend emit contracts needed before it can light up.
questions: What is the exact confirmed schema of ROLE_REGISTRY entries and C6 context_variables.REGISTRY entries that the render rules assume?
Will the cognition view be its own canvas, an overlay on the node canvas, or a rail panel?
Will role instances use the existing run://<turn>/<role> address or a new cog:// scheme?
How many past turns will the view retain and make replayable?
What are the final render tokens for cognition states and edge weights by cost?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is a design-surface rendering chapter for the concurrent-cognition layer that answers the vault's render-for-cognition open question.
apparent_themes: Live event stream as the render engine.
One source of truth, no second source in the frontend.
Navigable visual/spatial surface instead of text walls.
Honest accounting of unbuilt dependencies before frontend work.
Shared substrate for operator and RHM.
intention_signals: Make the concurrent-cognition layer visible as a live, interactive thought-graph so an operator and the RHM can see what roles fired, what was injected, and how attention/budget flowed per turn, while reusing existing canvas infrastructure and forcing backend data emission first.
novelty: Treats cognition (roles, chains, injections) as addressable registry data with explicit render rules and an emit-contract, rather than as prose or hidden internals; proposes a commander's bridge view where the brain node receives weighted injection edges from faculties and roles.
contribution: A render-rule mapping from cognition registry/event data to visual primitives, an inventory of existing canvas/SSE/registry machinery to reuse, a named backend-first emit contract for cognition.* events, and an open-question checklist for Tim.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a provisional design document specifying how to render the concurrent-cognition layer as a live, interactive node/edge thought-graph on the canvas, mapping registry-defined roles, chains, per-turn runs, injections, and destinations to visual primitives and identifying the backend emit contracts and frontend reuse needed.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to answer the vault's render-for-cognition open question by binding the cognition data model to the existing reflects-never-owns canvas infrastructure and surfacing what must still be built.
